description: The query is syntactically correct but slow. Add indexes and/or rewrite it to reduce cost.

scenarios.description: N+1 pattern, replace correlated subquery with JOIN
scenarios.schema_sql: CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, budget REAL); CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, dept_id INTEGER, name TEXT, salary REAL);
scenarios.data_sql: INSERT INTO departments VALUES (1,'Engineering',1000000),(2,'Marketing',500000),(3,'HR',300000); INSERT INTO employees SELECT i, (i%3)+1, 'Employee_'||i, 50000+(i*1000) FROM (WITH RECURSIVE cnt(i) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT i+1 FROM cnt WHERE i<100) SELECT i FROM cnt);
scenarios.slow_query: SELECT name, (SELECT name FROM departments d WHERE d.id = e.dept_id) as dept_name FROM employees e
scenarios.fast_query_example: SELECT e.name, d.name as dept_name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
scenarios.hint: Correlated subqueries execute once per row. Replace with a JOIN.

scenarios.description: Missing index on heavily filtered column
scenarios.schema_sql: CREATE TABLE orders (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, amount REAL, status TEXT, created_date TEXT);
scenarios.data_sql: INSERT INTO orders SELECT i, (i%200)+1, (i%50)+1, ROUND(10.0+(RANDOM()%1000)/10.0,2), CASE i%3 WHEN 0 THEN 'paid' WHEN 1 THEN 'pending' ELSE 'cancelled' END, date('2023-01-01','+' || (i%365) || ' days') FROM (WITH RECURSIVE cnt(i) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT i+1 FROM cnt WHERE i<5000) SELECT i FROM cnt);
scenarios.slow_query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'paid' AND created_date > '2024-01-01'
scenarios.fast_query_example: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'paid' AND created_date > '2024-01-01'
scenarios.recommended_index: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status_date ON orders(status, created_date)
scenarios.hint: Add a composite index on the columns used in WHERE clause.

scenarios.schema_sql: CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, sku TEXT, category TEXT, price REAL);
scenarios.data_sql: INSERT INTO products SELECT i, 'Product_'||i, 'SKU-'||(1000+i), CASE i%5 WHEN 0 THEN 'Electronics' WHEN 1 THEN 'Clothing' WHEN 2 THEN 'Books' WHEN 3 THEN 'Food' ELSE 'Home' END, ROUND(5.0+(RANDOM()%500)/10.0,2) FROM (WITH RECURSIVE cnt(i) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT i+1 FROM cnt WHERE i<2000) SELECT i FROM cnt);
scenarios.slow_query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%_100%' OR sku LIKE '%1100%'
scenarios.fast_query_example: SELECT * FROM products WHERE id IN (100, 1100) OR sku = 'SKU-1100'
scenarios.hint: Leading wildcards in LIKE prevent index usage. Consider exact matches or full-text search.
